transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: It is early autumn in Bellwether: cooler mornings, mild afternoons, mist and passing rain.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: The bus leaves you at Bellwether just after nine. Its engine noise thins along the road until the village is quiet again.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: For now, there is the road ahead, your suitcase at your feet, and Eleanor's key in your pocket.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Village Road.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The road has settled into a quieter interval.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mrs Ellis is repacking her shopping bag and checking the list.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Bellwether Bakery.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The bakery counter is staffed and warm with work.

transcript.speaker: Jonah
transcript.text: You must be Eleanor's relation. I had a loaf riding on whether you'd take the morning bus.

transcript.speaker: You
transcript.text: People talk about strangers here, then?

transcript.speaker: Jonah
transcript.text: Only until they stop being strangers. Around here, that rarely takes long.

transcript.speaker: Jonah
transcript.text: Here. Bread. Consider it an apology from the village gossip network.

transcript.speaker: Journal
transcript.text: Side Story completed: A Warm Welcome.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Village Road.

transcript.text: The road has settled into a quieter interval.

transcript.text: Delivery van is nearby, making morning deliveries.

transcript.text: You make your way to Bus Stop.

transcript.label: Walk down to the riverside path

transcript.speaker: Nearby
transcript.text: You catch part of a conversation.

transcript.speaker: Mara
transcript.text: Morning. You're out early.

transcript.speaker: Mrs Ellis
transcript.text: So are you. I thought I'd take the air while it's quiet.

transcript.speaker: Mara
transcript.text: It won't stay quiet for long.

transcript.speaker: Mrs Ellis
transcript.text: No. It never seems to, once everyone gets moving.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: The weather has shifted: heavy rain, 13°C, with brisk wind.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mara is asking a villager what they remember about the cottage garden.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mrs Ellis is walking at an unhurried pace across the green.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Riverside Path.

transcript.label: Follow the meadow path to the station

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Bellwether Halt.

transcript.label: Follow the meadow path to the river

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Riverside Path.

transcript.label: Climb back to the village green

transcript.text: You make your way to Village Green.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: There are 2 familiar villagers moving around the green.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mrs Ellis is heading home after finishing the morning errands.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: You make your way to Ashcroft Cottage.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Freshly disturbed soil and a growing cleared patch show that someone has been working in the garden.

transcript.speaker: Journal
transcript.text: Main Story completed: A House Left Waiting.

transcript.speaker: Journal
transcript.text: Main Story added: In Eleanor's Hand.

transcript.speaker: Eleanor's Letter
transcript.text: If you are reading this, then you came. Put the kettle on before you decide anything important. The house is kinder when it is warm.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: Below the signature, in different ink, is a final line: Do not worry if the bells wake you.

transcript.speaker: Journal
transcript.text: Main Story completed: In Eleanor's Hand.

transcript.speaker: Journal
transcript.text: Side Story added: The Cottage Garden.

transcript.speaker: Narrator
transcript.text: Rain works across the cottage in layers: roof, glass, gutter, leaves. For a while, every sound has an ordinary source.

transcript.speaker: Bellwether
transcript.text: Mara is checking the cottage garden boundary and hedges.
